text: A pointed peak with a belt across, I lead the alphabet without a loss. In this code, I take the lead, the very first step you'll need to read.
Two loops stacked upon a line, a buzzing insect shares my sign. I step into the second place, moving forward in this race.
A crescent moon facing the right, an ocean vast but out of sight. I sit right in the middle space, the third lock in this secret place.
A straight back with a rounded front, I start the day and lead the hunt. Penultimate is my domain, the fourth link in this sturdy chain.
Three teeth upon a vertical comb, I'm found at the end of every 'home'. I close this set, the final key, the fifth and last, unlocking thee.
A flag waving on a single pole, I am the spark that starts the 'fire'. I claim the very first position, starting off this secret mission.
An arrow spinning inward tight, the start of 'good' and end of 'dog'. I follow close behind the start, the second piece of this puzzle's heart.
Two tall walls and a bridge between, in 'heaven' and 'hell' I can be seen. I stand at number three, you see, the center of this mystery.
I am the ego, standing alone, a single pillar carved in stone. Number four is where I rest, one step away from finishing the test.
A fishhook waiting for a bite, I bring the 'joy' into the light. The fifth and final slot I claim, the ending of this guessing game.
A straight line with an open beak, I start the 'kite' that children seek. I am the first one in the queue, the starting point revealed to you.
A right angle sitting on the floor, the shape of a loser, but 'love's' true door. Second in line is where I fall, answering the puzzle's call.
Two mountain peaks that touch the sky, turned upside down, a 'W' am I. The third position is my throne, exactly in the middle zone.
A jagged peak, an endless 'no', I point the compass where to go. I take the fourth spot on the board, almost completing the final cord.
A perfect circle, an empty space, surprise is written on my face. The fifth and final number's mine, the last link in the line.
A head on a stick, looking to the right, a 'pirate's' start, prepared to fight. I am the vanguard, number one, the very place this code begun.
A circle with a tiny tail, without a 'U', I often fail. I hold the second spot in line, a quirky piece of this design.
A pirate's favorite sound to make, a leg kicks out for goodness' sake! Dead center at the number three, the heart of this security.
A winding river, a snake's swift glide, I am the plural at the end of the ride. Position four is where I stand, the second-to-last in this command.
A simple cross, a balanced scale, I end the 'cat' and start the 'tale'. Number five is my decree, the final cipher is the key.
An open cup to catch the rain, I sound like you but have no brain. I am the first to take the stage, the opening letter on this page.
A plunging valley, steep and deep, the Roman numeral five I keep. But here I sit at number two, the second step to see you through.
Two valleys joined or double 'U', I ask 'what', 'where', and 'who'. Third in the sequence is my place, right in the middle of the race.
I mark the spot where treasures hide, a crossing path from side to side. Position four is my domain, near the end of this brain-drain.
A branching road, a question asked, the end of 'day', a simple task. I finish up at number five, the last to keep this code alive.
A zigzag path, a sleepy sound, the final letter to be found. Yet here I break the natural rule, and stand first to test the fool.
A triangle with a missing base, the start of the human alphabet race. But here I take the second seat, the next step on this puzzling street.
The most common letter you will see, a horizontal pitchfork facing free. I sit at three, the middle bound, where balance in the code is found.
The seeing orb without the eye, a straight line pointing to the sky. I wait at four, almost the end, a subtle clue for you, my friend.
A ring of gold, a hollow sphere, when you understand, you say 'Oh dear!'. The fifth position is my home, the final stop where you will roam.
A loop on top of a single pole, I pop and ping to play my role. I follow the leader, taking slot two, revealing the next hidden step to you.
A straight-backed guard with an arm and a kick, I start the 'knight' though the 'n' does the trick. I stand at the center, the vital third space, the balancing act in this numeric race.
A sharp right angle, a corner turned square, I am the start of the 'love' in the air. Fourth in the sequence, I patiently wait, just one step away from unlocking the gate.
A circle left open, a crescent of light, I start off the 'cat' prowling into the night. I am the fifth, the final decree, the last puzzle piece to set the lock free.
Like a comb with two teeth or a flag blowing free, I start off the 'future' for you and for me. I am the vanguard, the very first key, unlocking the start of this mystery.
A horseshoe facing the heavens above, I sound like the person you're speaking of. Second in line, I step up to the plate, forging ahead through the cryptographic gate.
A straight wooden bow with a string pulled tight, I bring the 'dawn' after the dark of the night. Dead in the center, at index number three, I hold the middle of this secret key.
An open loop that curls inward and stops, I start the 'gravity' before the apple drops. Almost the end, I take the fourth slot, tightening the loop on the password's knot.
Up, then diagonal, and straight up once more, I sit at the center of the word 'ignore'. The fifth and the final, the last of the line, complete the sequence and the prize is thine.
A loop on a stick with a leg kicking out, I roar and I rumble, I whisper and shout. I stand at the threshold, the very first space, kicking things off in this puzzling chase.
Two bubbles attached to a vertical spine, I am the start of the 'borderline'. Seated at three, the heart of the code, exactly halfway down this winding road.
Twin mountain summits, a valley between, in 'magic' and 'mystery' I'm often seen. I slide into second, continuing the quest, putting your cipher skills to the test.
A flat wooden roof on a pillar so tall, I start off the 'time' that will govern us all. Fourth in the lineup, I stand straight and true, preparing the final transition for you.
A downward plunge and a steep upward climb, the shape of a wedge cutting right through the rhyme. The fifth position is where I descend, marking the cipher's definitive end.
Twin valleys carved out by rivers of old, I spin the 'web' where the secrets unfold. I am the alpha, the number one slot, the starting thread of this intricate plot.
An umbrella handle turned upside down, a jester who juggles to entertain town. I swing into second, the follow-up act, keeping the passkey entirely intact.
A perfect round circle with a tiny kickstand, the 'queen' of the letters ruling the land. I rest in the middle, position number three, the balancing point of the master key.
A slippery serpent that slithers around, a hissing 'silence' with barely a sound. Second-to-last is the spot that I claim, slot number four in this guessing game.
A fork in the road where you must make a choice, the roaring 'yell' of a powerful voice. The final position, the absolute end, slot number five is the message I send.
Like goalposts erected upon the green field, I am the 'hero' with sword and with shield. I step up to one, the very first gate, the opening move of this puzzle's fate.
